fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 12-year-old female brought by mother for mild situational anxiety before dance competition.
fields.Subjective: Patient is a 12-year-old female presenting with increased nervousness over the past week leading up to major dance competition this weekend. She reports butterflies in stomach and some trouble sleeping night before yesterday. She denies any thoughts of self-harm or suicidal ideation. Patient states she is excited but also worried about forgetting choreography or making mistakes. This is her first solo performance at a regional level. Mother reports patient has always experienced pre-performance jitters but they resolve once she begins dancing. She continues to attend practices enthusiastically and maintains normal appetite. Patient has successfully managed similar anxiety before smaller competitions using breathing techniques taught by dance instructor. She is socially active and doing well academically. (Nurse Practitioner Samantha White, PMHNP-BC)
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives with both parents; competitive dancer preparing for major performance; history of successfully managing pre-performance anxiety; socially engaged; academically successful; supportive family.
Assessment_and_Plan: Protective factors: strong support system, history of successful coping, passion for dance, good overall functioning, no suicidal ideation.
Reassurance provided regarding normalcy of pre-performance nerves.
Reviewed and reinforced previously successful anxiety management techniques.
Additional coping strategies discussed including visualization and progressive muscle relaxation.
Mother encouraged to provide support without adding pressure.
No treatment indicated; this is normal developmental response to performance situation.
Resources provided should anxiety generalize beyond performance situations.
fields.Author: Samantha White, PMHNP-BC (Nurse Practitioner)
